objective: Apply the BASIC/ADVANCED frontend mode contract to Automation Studio side-effecting and advanced workflow controls.
validationPlan: cd frontend; .\node_modules\.bin\playwright.cmd test tests/e2e/automation-studio.spec.ts tests/e2e/automation-builder.spec.ts tests/e2e/ui-mode.spec.ts --project=chromium --reporter=line
rollbackNotes: Preserve unrelated user changes; revert only this checkpoint scope if explicitly requested.
nextAction: Release frontend leases, run Codex validation, and continue with the next safe frontend slice if available.
outcome: Automation Studio activity authoring/execution controls and manual workflow trigger now use ADVANCED_ONLY mode metadata and are absent from BASIC mode. BASIC mode also blocks saving loaded draft-only journey nodes instead of silently dropping them.
validationRun: PASS: cd frontend; npm run lint
PASS: cd frontend; npm run build:ci
PASS: cd frontend; .\node_modules\.bin\playwright.cmd test tests/e2e/automation-studio.spec.ts tests/e2e/automation-builder.spec.ts tests/e2e/ui-mode.spec.ts --project=chromium --reporter=line
residualRisks: UI mode is not an authorization boundary; backend workflow and activity validation remains authoritative.
Template Studio still needs a separate BASIC/ADVANCED mode-metadata slice.
